The IGLL5 gene, also known as Immunoglobulin lambda-like polypeptide 5, is a gene that encodes for a protein involved in the development and function of B cells, which are a type of white blood cell that produces antibodies as part of the immune response.

The JCHAIN gene, also known as joining (J) chain, is a human gene that encodes a protein called the J chain. This gene is primarily expressed in B lymphocytes, where it plays a crucial role in the production and secretion of polymeric immunoglobulins, such as IgA and IgM.
